title: Futility pruning chooses a mate-losing rook move in a sparse promotion endgame
reference_note: The bad rook move allows a forced mate. The fixed move is Stockfish's depth-16 best defensive move and avoids the immediate mate, although Black remains worse.

before.baseline: pre-fix search-debug build before the futility guard

fix.summary: Futility pruning remains enabled, but depth-2 and depth-3 futility cutoffs are skipped in promotion-race and sparse-endgame nodes where static margins are not a safe bound for quiet mating or promotion resources.

root_cause.summary: The shallow futility cutoff used static evaluation plus a small margin in a sparse rook/pawn endgame with an immediate promotion race. In the refutation tree after Re1-b1, quiet promotion and mating resources are decisive, but the cutoff can return alpha before those resources are searched. Disabling futility alone removed the bad move; other individual pruning toggles did not remove it at both reproduced depths.
